reasoning: The Cleveland Fed's 1-year real interest rate series (REAINTRATREARAT1YE) is highly volatile, with month-to-month changes often exceeding 0.4%. While the over-arching trend since late 2024 has been negative (reflecting Federal Reserve rate cuts and easing nominal yields), the most recent October 2025 value (0.973) is quite low compared to the 1-year average (1.48) and the 6-month average (~1.18). Historically, drops below 1.0 in this series (e.g., in Apr and Jul 2025) were immediately followed by strong mean-reverting bounces the following month. Based on the statistical trend and seasonality analysis, there is an approximate 55% probability that the next observation will be higher than the mid-October level. For the January 2026 resolution date, the forecast horizon is further out; the continued underlying downward drift of nominal policy rates slightly dampens the probability of an increase, though mean reversion limits how far it will fall. Following the instruction to have probabilities closer to 0.50 for more distant dates, the probability for Jan 2026 is slightly reduced to 0.53.
